entries.definition:
كتاب الباء

بعد

* ونَصِيُّ باعِجةٍ ومَحْضٌ مُنْقَعُ *

قال أبو زياد: [و] أبو فقعس: الباعجة الرُّحَيْبَة الصغيرة بَعَجَتِ الوادِي من أحَدِ جانبَيْه؛ وهي مِن مَنابت النّصيّ. ويقال الباعِجة آخرُ الرَّمل، مكانٌ بين السّهل والحَزْن، رُبما كان مرتفِعاً وربما كان مُنْحَدِراً. قال النَّضر: الباعجة مكان مطمئنٌّ من الرِّمال كهيئة الغائط، أرض مَدْكوكة لا أسناد لها، تُنبت الرِّمْث والحَمْضَ* وأطايب العُشْب.

وكلُّ ما تَرَكْنَاهُ من هذا الجِنْس كنَحو ما ذَكرناه . وباعِجة القِرْدان مَوضِعٌ في قول أوس:

* فباعِجةِ القِرْدان فالمُتَثَلِّمِ *

(بعد) الباء والعين والدال أصلان: خِلاَفُ القُرْبِ، ومُقابِلُ قَبْل. قالوا: البُعْد خلاف القُرْب، والبُعْد والبَعَد الهلاك. وقالوا في قوله تعالى: ] كما بَعِدَتْ ثَمُودُ [ [هود 95]، أي هَلَكَت. وقياسُ ذلك واحدٌ. والأباعد خلاف الأقارب. قال:

يُريب مِن الأدْنَى رَمَاكَ الأباعدُ

إذا أنْتَ لم تَعْرُكْ بِجَنْبِكَ بَعْضَ ما

وتقول: تَنَحَّ غير باعِدٍ، أي غيرَ صاغر. وتَنَحَّ غير بَعيدٍ، أي كُن قريباً.

وأمّا الآخرُ فقولك جاء مِنْ بَعْدُ، كما تقولُ في خلافِهِ: مِن قَبْلُ.

بعر - بعص - بعض

b-ʿ-d to be far, to go far; to come next; to remove, to separate.
Of this root, seven forms occur 235 times in the Qurʾan: بَعُدَ
baʿuda once; بَعِدَ baʿida once; [[unclear: بَاعِد / baʿid]] once; بُعْد
buʿd seven times; بَعِيد baʿīd 25 times; مُبْعَدُون mubʿadūn once and بَعْد
baʿd 199 times.

بَعُدَ baʿuda u [v. intrans.] to be or become distant, remote, far
off; to be far away, to go very far (9:42) لَوْ كَانَ عَرَضًا قَرِيبًا وَسَفَرًا قَاصِدًا
لَاتَّبَعُوكَ وَلَكِنْ بَعُدَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الشُّقَّةُ were it a gain near at hand and an easy

101 ب/ع/د

journey, they would have followed you, but the distance had
proved far for them.

بَعِدَ baʿida a [v. intrans.] to perish, to come to destruction
(11:95) أَلَا بُعْدًا لِمَدْيَنَ كَمَا بَعِدَتْ ثَمُودُ yes!, away with the people of
Midian, even as Thamūd was done away with.

بُعْد buʿd I [n.] remoteness, distance (43:38) يَالَيْتَ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكَ بُعْدَ
الْمَشْرِقَيْنِ if only there were between me and you the distance
between east and west! II [v. n., interjectionally] ‘away with...!’,
‘destruction to...!’ (11:60) أَلَا بُعْدًا لِّعَادٍ قَوْمِ هُودٍ so away with ʿād, the
people of Hūd!

بَعِيد baʿīd [quasi-act. part.] 1 distant, far away (50:31) وَأُزْلِفَتِ
الْجَنَّةُ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ غَيْرَ بَعِيدٍ and Paradise is brought closer for the
righteous—not very far 2 long, distant in time (27:22) فَمَكَثَ غَيْرَ بَعِيدٍ
but he did not stay away long 3 deep, far-reaching (2:176) وَإِنَّ الَّذِينَ
اخْتَلَفُوا فِي الْكِتَابِ لَفِي شِقَاقٍ بَعِيدٍ those who differ about the Scripture are
deeply divided 4 considerable (42:18) أَلَا إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُمَارُونَ فِي
السَّاعَةِ لَفِي ضَلَالٍ بَعِيدٍ those who argue about the Hour are truly in
considerable misguidedness.

مُبْعَدُون mubʿadūn [pl. of pass. part. مُبْعَد mubʿad] kept away,
removed, debarred (21:101) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ سَبَقَتْ لَهُمْ مِنَّا الْحُسْنَى أُولَٰئِكَ عَنْهَا
مُبْعَدُونَ but those for whom [a promise of] the reward most fair has
been already given by Us, they will be kept away from it [Hell].

بَعْد baʿd [adverb of time and place, always in construct (إضافة),
sometimes elliptically] 1 after, afterwards (2:253) وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ مَا اقْتَتَلَ
الَّذِينَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَتْهُمُ الْبَيِّنَاتُ but if God had so willed, their
successors would not have fought each other after they had been
brought clear signs 2 in addition to (21:105) وَلَقَدْ كَتَبْنَا فِي الزَّبُورِ مِنْ بَعْدِ
الذِّكْرِ أَنَّ الْأَرْضَ يَرِثُهَا عِبَادِيَ الصَّالِحُونَ We wrote in the Psalms, in
addition to the Remembrance that the earth shall be the
inheritance of My righteous servants (or, We wrote in the Psalms,
after the [earlier] Scripture that the earth shall be the inheritance
of My righteous servants) 3 apart from, beyond (10:32) فَذَٰلِكُمُ اللَّهُ
رَبُّكُمُ الْحَقُّ فَمَاذَا بَعْدَ الْحَقِّ إِلَّا الضَّلَالُ so that is God, your Lord, the True One
(or, the Truth), what is there, beyond the Truth, except falsehood?
4 over and above (31:27) وَلَوْ أَنَّمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ مِنْ شَجَرَةٍ أَقْلَامٌ وَالْبَحْرُ يَمُدُّهُ مِنْ

102 ب/ع/د

بَعْدِهِ سَبْعَةُ أَبْحُرٍ مَّا نَفِدَتْ كَلِمَاتُ اللَّهِ even if all the trees on earth were
pens and the sea with seven seas over and above to replenish it,
[were ink], the words of God would not run out 5 because of, in
consequence of (66:4) فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ مَوْلَاهُ وَجِبْرِيلُ وَصَالِحُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
وَالْمَلَائِكَةُ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ ظَهِيرٌ but if you collaborate against him, [be
warned that] God will aid him, as will Gabriel and all the
righteous believers, and the angels, because of that, will stand
behind him 6 in spite of, notwithstanding, even after (5:32) وَلَقَدْ
جَاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُنَا بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ ثُمَّ إِنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنْهُم بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ فِي الْأَرْضِ لَمُسْرِفُونَ
and indeed Our messengers came to them with clear signs, but many of them,
in spite of this, commit excesses in the land 7 after, apart from
(45:23) أَفَرَأَيْتَ مَنِ اتَّخَذَ إِلَٰهَهُ هَوَاهُ وَأَضَلَّهُ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ عِلْمٍ وَخَتَمَ عَلَىٰ
سَمْعِهِ وَقَلْبِهِ وَجَعَلَ عَلَىٰ بَصَرِهِ غِشَاوَةً فَمَن يَهْدِيهِ مِن بَعْدِ اللَّهِ
consider the one who takes his own desire as a [controlling] deity, and God leads him astray,
inspite of [his/God’s] knowledge, and sets a seal upon his
hearing and his heart, and places a covering on his eyes—who can
guide him after (or, apart from) God? 8 before (in a disputed
interpretation of 21:105) وَلَقَدْ كَتَبْنَا فِي الزَّبُورِ مِنْ بَعْدِ الذِّكْرِ أَنَّ الْأَرْضَ يَرِثُهَا
عِبَادِيَ الصَّالِحُونَ We wrote in the Psalms, before the [earlier]
Scripture/Remembrance: ‘The earth shall be the inheritance of
My righteous servants’; (79:30) وَالْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ دَحَاهَا and the Earth,
before (or, after) that, He spread it out.
entries.definition_english:
b-ʿ-d to be far, to go far; to come next; to remove, to separate.
Of this root, seven forms occur 235 times in the Qurʾan: بَعُدَ
baʿuda once; بَعِدَ baʿida once; [[unclear: بَاعِد / baʿid]] once; بُعْد
buʿd seven times; بَعِيد baʿīd 25 times; مُبْعَدُون mubʿadūn once and بَعْد
baʿd 199 times.

بَعُدَ baʿuda u [v. intrans.] to be or become distant, remote, far
off; to be far away, to go very far (9:42) لَوْ كَانَ عَرَضًا قَرِيبًا وَسَفَرًا قَاصِدًا
لَاتَّبَعُوكَ وَلَكِنْ بَعُدَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الشُّقَّةُ were it a gain near at hand and an easy

101 ب/ع/د

journey, they would have followed you, but the distance had
proved far for them.

بَعِدَ baʿida a [v. intrans.] to perish, to come to destruction
(11:95) أَلَا بُعْدًا لِمَدْيَنَ كَمَا بَعِدَتْ ثَمُودُ yes!, away with the people of
Midian, even as Thamūd was done away with.

بُعْد buʿd I [n.] remoteness, distance (43:38) يَالَيْتَ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكَ بُعْدَ
الْمَشْرِقَيْنِ if only there were between me and you the distance
between east and west! II [v. n., interjectionally] ‘away with...!’,
‘destruction to...!’ (11:60) أَلَا بُعْدًا لِّعَادٍ قَوْمِ هُودٍ so away with ʿād, the
people of Hūd!

بَعِيد baʿīd [quasi-act. part.] 1 distant, far away (50:31) وَأُزْلِفَتِ
الْجَنَّةُ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ غَيْرَ بَعِيدٍ and Paradise is brought closer for the
righteous—not very far 2 long, distant in time (27:22) فَمَكَثَ غَيْرَ بَعِيدٍ
but he did not stay away long 3 deep, far-reaching (2:176) وَإِنَّ الَّذِينَ
اخْتَلَفُوا فِي الْكِتَابِ لَفِي شِقَاقٍ بَعِيدٍ those who differ about the Scripture are
deeply divided 4 considerable (42:18) أَلَا إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُمَارُونَ فِي
السَّاعَةِ لَفِي ضَلَالٍ بَعِيدٍ those who argue about the Hour are truly in
considerable misguidedness.

مُبْعَدُون mubʿadūn [pl. of pass. part. مُبْعَد mubʿad] kept away,
removed, debarred (21:101) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ سَبَقَتْ لَهُمْ مِنَّا الْحُسْنَى أُولَٰئِكَ عَنْهَا
مُبْعَدُونَ but those for whom [a promise of] the reward most fair has
been already given by Us, they will be kept away from it [Hell].

بَعْد baʿd [adverb of time and place, always in construct (إضافة),
sometimes elliptically] 1 after, afterwards (2:253) وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ مَا اقْتَتَلَ
الَّذِينَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَتْهُمُ الْبَيِّنَاتُ but if God had so willed, their
successors would not have fought each other after they had been
brought clear signs 2 in addition to (21:105) وَلَقَدْ كَتَبْنَا فِي الزَّبُورِ مِنْ بَعْدِ
الذِّكْرِ أَنَّ الْأَرْضَ يَرِثُهَا عِبَادِيَ الصَّالِحُونَ We wrote in the Psalms, in
addition to the Remembrance that the earth shall be the
inheritance of My righteous servants (or, We wrote in the Psalms,
after the [earlier] Scripture that the earth shall be the inheritance
of My righteous servants) 3 apart from, beyond (10:32) فَذَٰلِكُمُ اللَّهُ
رَبُّكُمُ الْحَقُّ فَمَاذَا بَعْدَ الْحَقِّ إِلَّا الضَّلَالُ so that is God, your Lord, the True One
(or, the Truth), what is there, beyond the Truth, except falsehood?
4 over and above (31:27) وَلَوْ أَنَّمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ مِنْ شَجَرَةٍ أَقْلَامٌ وَالْبَحْرُ يَمُدُّهُ مِنْ

102 ب/ع/د

بَعْدِهِ سَبْعَةُ أَبْحُرٍ مَّا نَفِدَتْ كَلِمَاتُ اللَّهِ even if all the trees on earth were
pens and the sea with seven seas over and above to replenish it,
[were ink], the words of God would not run out 5 because of, in
consequence of (66:4) فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ مَوْلَاهُ وَجِبْرِيلُ وَصَالِحُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ

warned that] God will aid him, as will Gabriel and all the
righteous believers, and the angels, because of that, will stand
behind him 6 in spite of, notwithstanding, even after (5:32) وَلَقَدْ
جَاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُنَا بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ ثُمَّ إِنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنْهُم بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ فِي الْأَرْضِ لَمُسْرِفُونَ

hearing and his heart, and places a covering on his eyes—who can
guide him after (or, apart from) God? 8 before (in a disputed
interpretation of 21:105) وَلَقَدْ كَتَبْنَا فِي الزَّبُورِ مِنْ بَعْدِ الذِّكْرِ أَنَّ الْأَرْضَ يَرِثُهَا
عِبَادِيَ الصَّالِحُونَ We wrote in the Psalms, before the [earlier]
Scripture/Remembrance: ‘The earth shall be the inheritance of
My righteous servants’; (79:30) وَالْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ دَحَاهَا and the Earth,
before (or, after) that, He spread it out.
